svg.text: Domestic + International Pharmaceutical Standards with Cross-Reference Comparison Table
Pharmacopoeia of the People's Republic of China
4900+ monographs · General chapters (0400 series for ID, 0500 for assay)
Published by: China Medical Science Press · Official: NMPA
GMP (2010 Revision) + 6 Appendices (sterile, non-sterile, biological, blood, etc.)
Annex 1: Sterile Products · Annex 2: Biological · Annex 3: API · Annex 4: Blood Products
Covers: Toxicity, pharmacokinetics, safety pharmacology, mutagenicity
Drug Administration Law (2019 Rev.) · NMPA Technical Guidelines
Drug Registration Regulation · Clinical Trial Management · MAH System
USP: <711> Dissolution, <71> Sterility, <467> Residual Solvents, <1058> Instrument Qualification
EP: 2.9.3 Dissolution, 2.6.1 Sterility, 2.4.24 Residual Solvents — harmonized with ICH
WHO-GMP · FDA cGMP (21 CFR 210/211) · EU-GMP (EudraLex Vol. 4)
EU-GMP Annex 1: Sterile (2022 Rev.) · FDA Aseptic Processing Guidance
PIC/S: Pharmaceutical Inspection Co-operation Scheme (global harmonization)
OECD Test Guidelines: TG 408 (90-day oral), TG 423 (acute oral), TG 471 (Ames test)
OECD MAD (Mutual Acceptance of Data) — recognized in 38+ countries
International Council for Harmonisation of Technical Requirements
Q-Series: Q1 (Stability), Q2 (Validation), Q3 (Impurities), Q7 (GMP API), Q8-Q11 (QbD)
S-Series: S7A/S7B (Safety Pharmacology), S9 (Oncology) · E-Series: Clinical Efficacy
WHO Lab Biosafety Manual · CDC BMBL
How the same analytical test is specified across different pharmacopoeias
The simulator automatically references the applicable standard clause at each simulation stage
Dual-standard mode: students see both ChP and USP/EP requirements side-by-side for each operation
OpenClaw Skill v1.0 | Dual-Standard Compliance Matrix | MIT License

ChP = Chinese Pharmacopoeia | USP = United States Pharmacopeia | EP = European Pharmacopoeia | ICH = International Council for Harmonisation | GMP = Good Manufacturing Practice | GLP = Good Laboratory Practice | NMPA = National Medical Products Administration | GHS = Globally Harmonized System | EHS = Environment, Health & Safety | OSHA = Occupational Safety and Health Administration | 3R = Replace, Reduce, Refine | IACUC = Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee | AVMA = American Veterinary Medical Association
